sections.label: Verse 1
lines: Soft light through the window,
and I still can't believe you're here.
All the years I waited,
they melt the moment you're near.
(oh)
I was scared of falling,
scared of all I couldn't see —
but how could I be afraid
when love like this found me?
So I'm walking to you now,
every step, my whole life through.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: I have waited six thousand years
just to hold you here tonight.
I will love you past the turning of the stars,
past the fading of the light.
Take my hand, I'm not afraid,
every road has led me true —
and I'll spend forever, love,
just learning how to treasure you.

sections.label: Verse 2
lines: Here beneath the promise,
here where all our people stand,
every year that led me to you
was held inside His hand.
(mmm)
Let the mountains turn to valleys,
let the ocean lose its shore —
this love is old as morning,
and it only asks for more.
So I give you every sunrise,
every road I'm yet to know.

sections.label: Chorus
lines: I have waited six thousand years
just to hold you here tonight.
I will love you past the turning of the stars,
past the fading of the light.
Take my hand, I'm not afraid,
every road has led me true —
and I'll spend forever, love,
just learning how to treasure you.
Every star was leaning toward this moment,
God has drawn your heart to mine;
through six thousand years, through every winter,
you were always meant to shine.

sections.label: Final Chorus
lines: I have waited six thousand years
just to hold you here tonight.
I will love you past the turning of the stars,
past the fading of the light.
Take my hand, I'm not afraid,
every road has led me true —
and I'll spend forever, love,
just learning how to treasure you.

sections.label: Outro
lines: Six thousand years...
(all my life, to you)
